assistant_message:
Fix the paid-acquisition path first, specifically the STR Design Diagnostic as the front-end step into the full-service project. I would not treat CAC as the current bottleneck: the known economics are about $1,000 CAC against about $10,000 first-30-day gross profit, and the Money Models frame says CAC, gross profit, and payback period are the acquisition numbers to triangulate [how-businesses-make-money:2]. Payback gets easier to scale when gross profit gets back over CAC quickly [payback-period:0], and 30-day gross profit above CAC is the core client-financed acquisition threshold [cfa:0].

So my first priority is not adding another back-end product; it is proving that the $597 diagnostic can turn cold or paid traffic into qualified premium design conversations. Keep this as one simple money-model move before adding complexity [make-your-money-model:2]. Run a small paid test, measure diagnostic booked-call rate, close rate into the $15k full-service offer, and actual CAC by channel. If the diagnostic creates qualified calls but conversion is weak, fix the diagnostic promise/sales process. If CAC rises above first-30-day gross profit, then acquisition becomes the constraint.
